title: Phyllodoce alata
taxonomy: Phyllodoce alata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to the western United States and is often found in alpine and subalpine regions. This plant is also known by its synonym, Phyllodoce empetriformis. Phyllodoce alata is a low-growing, shrub-like plant with white or pink flowers. It is typically found growing in rocky, well-drained areas with full sun to partial shade.

intro: Phyllodoce alata, also known as the pink mountain heather, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Phyllodoce alata include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Phyllodoce alata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used to make topical creams and salves for skin care applications.
